title: 10 best herbal creams for swollen gums

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil has a natural cooling effect that reduces swelling and discomfort by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat the bacteria that cause gum disease, further alleviating symptoms like redness and tenderness. By soothing and calming the gums, peppermint creams provide relief from swollen, irritated gums, promoting a healthier smile.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Peppermint cream's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthier oral environment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint cream, such as menthol and methanol, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums, providing relief from discomfort.

Astringent properties: Peppermint cream's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can occur due to gum inflammation or injury, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, peppermint roots are used to create a cooling effect that helps reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint leaves help soothe and calm inflamed tissues, making them an effective ingredient in creams for swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by submerging them in boiling water for 15 minutes before use.
Combine 20g of dried peppermint leaves with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heatproof glass jar.
Heat the mixture using a double boiler method for 10-15 minutes or until the oil reaches a temperature of 70°c to 80°c.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another sterile glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix 100ml of beeswax with 300ml of the prepared peppermint-infused oil and heat gently over low heat until the wax is fully melted and the mixture forms a smooth, consistent cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with swollen gums because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and discomfort. The herbs present in these creams, such as sage leaf extract, have been traditionally used to soothe gum inflammation and promote healing. Additionally, sage's antioxidant compounds help combat bacterial growth, further contributing to the reduction of puffiness and pain associated with swollen gums. As a result, using herbal sage creams can provide fast and effective relief for those experiencing gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sage cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling by killing or inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling, redness, and pain in affected gums.

Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the appearance of swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the sage plant contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or oven drying at 30-40°c for 1-2 hours, reducing moisture content.
Grind 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 50ml of cold-pressed coconut oil with the ground herbal powder in a heat-resistant glass beaker, stirring until well mixed, then cover and infuse at room temperature for 2 hours.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and storing the infused cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months, with suggested use as a topical treatment for swollen gums 3-4 times daily.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Kansas coneflower creams, such as triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antibacterial properties: The cream's antibacterial activity helps to combat oral bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and infection, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and reducing the risk of complications.

Wound healing acceleration: The wound-healing compounds in Kansas coneflower creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, accelerate the repair process by improving blood flow, collagen synthesis, and tissue regeneration, which helps to reduce gum swelling and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum inflammation.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have natural astringent properties that can soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until crumbly.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered echinacea angustifolia with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 10 minutes as it cools to room temperature, then pour into sterilized containers.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with swollen gums because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called echinacea, which has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The cream's soothing properties help to calm irritated gum tissue, reducing inflammation and redness while promoting healing. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize harmful free radicals that can contribute to gum damage, making it an effective natural remedy for swollen gums.

Pot marigold creams helps with swollen gums because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as calophyllum and camphor, work to soothe irritated gum tissue and promote a healthy environment for healing. Additionally, the cream's gentle, non-irritating properties allow it to penetrate deep into the gum pocket, providing targeted relief from swollen gums, redness, and pain. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to combat gum inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby alleviating swelling.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce gum swelling, pain, and redness.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Pot marigold's mucilages and polysaccharides in the cream create a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritated gums, promoting wound healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Buds: The buds of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Grind dried calendula flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder until well pulverized.
Mix powdered calendula with 20 grams of beeswax and 40 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture frequently as it cools to a temperature around 120 degrees fahrenheit and add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Pour the warm mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia creams helps with swollen gums because of its unique properties that combat inflammation and soothe irritated tissues. The herbal extract contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, reducing swelling and redness in the affected areas. Additionally, stevia's natural sweetness can help to mask any unpleasant tastes or odors associated with gum disease, providing a calming effect on the gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stevia cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, leading to a decrease in inflammation.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in stevia cream, including polyphenols and saponins, help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting overall oral health.

Wound-healing properties: Stevia has been shown to have wound-healing properties, which can aid in the repair of damaged gum tissue and promote faster healing, reducing the appearance of swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, stevia roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe swollen gums.
Barks: Stevia barks contain compounds like stevioside and rebaudioside, which can reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stevia rebaudiana leaves and dry them in shade for 3-4 days.
Combine dried stevia leaves with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler at 120°f.
Stir mixture until beeswax melts and mixture thickens to desired consistency within 15 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and tea tree essential oil to the cream base and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and seal tightly, allowing it to cool at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains a unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gum tissue. When applied topically, aloe cream can help soothe irritated gums, reducing redness and inflammation, and promoting healthy gum function. Its gentle and non-irritating properties make it an effective natural remedy for relieving swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain in swollen gums by decreasing inflammation and alleviating discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of aloe vera, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help combat gum infections that can cause swelling and promote healing of damaged tissues.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera's natural soothing and moisturizing properties accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and enhancing the regrowth of healthy gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antibacterial properties, helping to combat infection and promote gum healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves weighing approximately 200 grams and wash thoroughly under running water.
Extract the gel from the aloe vera leaves using a grater or blender preparing 100 milliliters of pure aloe vera gel.
Combine 20 milliliters of coconut oil with 10 milliliters of beeswax pellets melting the mixture in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the extracted aloe vera gel to the melted oil and wax mixture stirring thoroughly until fully incorporated within five minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with swollen gums because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties from herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and tea tree oil. These ingredients work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of herbal teas also help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum disease. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of herbal teas help to ease discomfort and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Antimicrobial properties: Herbs like sage, echinacea, and goldenseal contain antimicrobial agents that combat bacteria and viruses causing gum infections, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Astringent and soothing properties: Herbal teas like tea tree, licorice root, and marshmallow root have astringent and soothing effects that help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and protect the gums from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial properties, effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and rinse them thoroughly in cold water.
Combine 20 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves with 400 milliliters of distilled water for steeping.
Steep the mixture at a temperature of 85 degrees celsius for exactly 30 minutes to extract optimal bioactive compounds.
Strain and filter the resulting liquid through cheesecloth to produce a clear herbal extract.
Combine the extracted liquid with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax, heating the mixture at 65 degrees celsius for precisely 45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bigleaf hydrangea creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and pain in various parts of the body, including the mouth. When applied topically to the affected area, bigleaf hydrangea cream can help to alleviate discomfort and stiffness in the gums, making it easier to eat and speak normally. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties can also help to combat gum infections that may be contributing to the swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Bigleaf hydrangea contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, a type of inflammatory mediator.

Antibacterial properties: The cream may contain antibacterial compounds like salicin, which can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum inflammation and disease by disrupting the cell membranes of bacteria and preventing their growth.

Antioxidant activity: Bigleaf hydrangea's antioxidant properties can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, bigleaf hydrangea roots are used to reduce swelling and inflammation in gum tissue.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, bigleaf hydrangea barks help to soothe and calm irritated gum tissue, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried hydrangea macrophylla flowers and 1 cup of distilled water for infusion.
Steep the herbs in hot water for 10 minutes to extract the active compounds and create a strong tea.
Combine 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 4 tablespoons of infused herbal liquid in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency after 10-15 minutes.
Pour the warm cream into clean, sterilized jars and seal them tightly, allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated gum tissues. The natural extracts in licorice root cream, such as glycyrrhizin, reduce inflammation and pain associated with gum swelling, providing instant relief. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial growth that can cause gum inflammation, allowing for a healthier oral environment. As a result, licorice creams offer an effective and natural solution to alleviate swollen gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Licoricidin: This bioactive compound exhibits antimicrobial activity, which can help to combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum disease and swelling, while also promoting wound healing.

Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from further damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce swelling and soothe gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat gum diseases due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried licorice root extract of glycyrrhiza glabra for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Combine 20 grams of the dried licorice root extract with 100 milliliters of distilled water and steep for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove any solids and obtain a clear liquid extract.
Mix 10 grams of vaseline petroleum jelly with 5 grams of coconut oil and slowly add the filtered licorice root extract while stirring continuously.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool before sealing and storing at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with swollen gums because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients, which work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort. The cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause swollen gums, while its anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm the affected area. Additionally, the cream's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and promoting healthy gum tissue regeneration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum (Eucalyptus globulus) has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums due to its ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause swollen gums, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting healing.
Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antioxidant compounds help protect gum tissue from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in affected areas.
Barks: The barks are rich in tannins, which have antiseptic properties that aid in healing wounds and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality eucalyptus globulus essential oil, coconut oil, beeswax, and distilled water in separate containers.
Melt 50g of beeswax in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds at a time, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Combine 10ml of eucalyptus globulus essential oil with 20ml of coconut oil in a small bowl and stir well to combine.
Add the melted beeswax to the oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated, then slowly add 40ml of distilled water while stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into clean glass jars, allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root cause of gum swelling. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and ursolic acid, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums. Additionally, dandelion's natural antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum swelling. By soothing and calming the affected area, dandelion creams provide effective relief for those suffering from gum inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion contains compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it an effective remedy for reducing swelling in the gums.

Antimicrobial: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of dandelion can help combat oral infections that may be contributing to swollen gums, promoting a healthy mouth environment and preventing further infection.

Healing and soothing: Dandelion's high mucilage content provides a protective coating for the mucous membranes in the mouth, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissue, reducing discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Buds: Containing bioactive flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of cream.
Decoct dried taraxacum officinale ingredients in boiling water for 30 minutes then strain the liquid.
Combine strained taraxacum officinale infusion with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax at 160°f.
Whip the cooled and solidified cream mixture into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer or whisk.
Pour the prepared taraxacum officinale cream into sterilized containers and allow it to set for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with swollen gums because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds help to constrict blood vessels, which decreases swelling and bleeding in the gums. Additionally, St John's Wort creams may also stimulate the healing process by increasing collagen synthesis, promoting tissue repair, and reducing bacterial growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort in the gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in St. John's Wort cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum inflammation.

Antibacterial properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain oral bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, which is a common cause of periodontal disease and swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of flavonoids and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swollen gums.
Leaves: The leaves of St John's Wort are rich in tannins, which provide astringent and anti-inflammatory effects when used to soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and dry them for at least four hours in a cool dark place.
Grind one part dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix the powdered herb with two parts coconut oil and one part beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Heat the mixture for about 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain salicylic acid, a derivative of aspirin that is naturally present in roses, which effectively reduces swelling and alleviates pain. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to reduce redness and discomfort associated with swollen gums. The gentle and non-irritating nature of damask rose creams makes it an effective and comfortable solution for those seeking natural relief from gum inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Damask rose contains compounds like phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenes that have antibacterial activity, which helps to combat bacterial infections that may be causing gum swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-carotene and vitamin E, reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in swollen gums, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Damask rose's natural antioxidants and flavonoids accelerate wound healing by improving blood circulation, reducing swelling, and enhancing collagen synthesis, which helps to soothe and repair damaged gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in swollen gums.
Fruits: Damask rose fruits, also known as hips, are rich in antioxidants and vitamins A and C, which promote gum health and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa damascena flowers and dry them in the shade for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried rosa damascena flowers with 500ml of hot distilled water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until melted and smooth.
Combine the herbal infusion with the melted wax-oil mixture, stirring well to emulsify, and pour into sterilized jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's soothing essence, derived from the calming herb Lavandula angustifolia, penetrates deep into the gum tissue to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Wound-healing property: Lavender's ability to accelerate wound healing can aid in the recovery of swollen gums by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, reducing the risk of further infection.

remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard solids immediately.
Mix 100g of beeswax pellets with 50ml of the lavender infusion and heat until melted at 70°c to 80°c.
Combine 20ml of coconut oil and 10ml of sweet almond oil and stir until well mixed with the warm lavender-infused beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool for at least 30 minutes before sealing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint creams helps with swollen gums because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The menthol in spearmint relaxes the gum tissue, allowing for improved blood flow and reducing redness. Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in spearmint cream help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum disease, reducing the severity of swollen gums and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Spearmint's wound-healing properties, attributed to its high levels of antioxidants and vitamins A and C, aid in the recovery process by promoting collagen production, tissue repair, and reduced scar formation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves of mentha spicata and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 20 grams of dried mint leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 24 hours at room temperature, then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 30 grams of coconut oil and heat them together until melted and well combined.
Add the infused mint extract to the melted wax-oil mixture and stir well before pouring it into tin tubes for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's active compounds, including acetic acid and sesquiterpenes, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, yarrow contains antioxidants that help combat free radicals and promote healing in the affected area. As a result, using herbal yarrow creams can provide effective relief from swollen gums, promoting healthy oral tissues and a reduced risk of further complications.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow cream, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help prevent infections that can cause gum swelling and promote healing.
Astringent properties: Yarrow's astringent properties help reduce bleeding and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and reducing excess mucus production in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation.
Buds: Yarrow buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial activity, making them effective against bacteria that cause periodontal disease and promoting healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and dry them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours or use dried leaves that have been stored properly.
Combine 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth to create the final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with swollen gums because they contain flavonoids and terpenes, which possess potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, these compounds work to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and combat infection. Fennel's natural antibacterial agents also help eliminate bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and combating bacterial growth, fennel creams provide a natural solution for soothing swollen gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and lignans that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of further complications.

Digestive soothing compounds: Fennel creams often contain digestive soothing compounds like mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the mouth and throat, while also aiding digestion and alleviating symptoms like bloating and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the fennel plant contain compounds like anethole, which has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat gum infections that cause swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 6 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture for 10-15 minutes until melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 160°f, then add 4 drops of peppermint essential oil and mix well for 30 seconds.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate at a temperature below 40°f for at least 2 hours or overnight to solidify the cream.
Once solidified, use the foeniculum vulgare creams as needed, applying a small amount topically to affected areas of gums two to three times daily for optimal results within 7-10 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The antiseptic and astringent qualities of lemongrass also help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of lemongrass cream provide relief from pain and discomfort, allowing for improved oral hygiene and reduced risk of further infection. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective solution for swollen gums, promoting healthy and comfortable teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, a common cause of gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemongrass, including kaempferol and quercetin, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage in the mouth, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing the severity of swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems (rhyzomes) of lemongrass contain antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered herb with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and steep for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the herbal extract with 200g of petroleum jelly and 20ml of coconut oil in a double boiler until fully incorporated and smooth for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Marjoram contains compounds like cineole, borneol, and beta-pinene that work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. These natural ingredients also have antimicrobial properties, which help combat gum infections and prevent further inflammation. When applied topically as a cream, marjoram's soothing properties can quickly alleviate discomfort and promote healing, providing relief from swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as terpenes and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums by decreasing prostaglandins production.
Antimicrobial property: Marjoram's antimicrobial activity helps combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections, reducing the risk of further inflammation and promoting healing.

Analgesic and antispasmodic property: Marjoram's analgesic and antispasmodic properties help relieve pain, discomfort, and stiffness in the gums, making it easier to eat, speak, and perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet marjoram roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Flowers: Marjoram flowers have antibacterial properties, which can prevent gum infections and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves and measure out 2 tablespoons for the cream preparation.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1 cup of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding solids after 5 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 5 minutes.
Combine the strained plant infusion with the beeswax-coconut oil mixture and whip until smooth, allowing to cool at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for swollen gums are topical preparations made from natural extracts of herbs, plants, and spices that provide relief from gum inflammation and swelling. These creams help to reduce pain, discomfort, and bleeding by promoting healing, soothing irritations, and fighting off bacterial infections. Examples include creams containing turmeric, clove, and tea tree oil, which have anti-inflammatory properties that ease gum swelling and promote a healthy environment for oral tissues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal creams is by using a combination of potent herbs known for their anti-inflammatory properties. Aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula are popular choices that help reduce swelling and ease pain. Apply the cream directly to the affected area 2-3 times a day, gently massaging it in until fully absorbed. For optimal results, use a herbal cream with a concentration of at least 20% active ingredients.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal creams are various skin issues, including eczema, acne, and rosacea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile can help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and discomfort. These natural remedies may also be used to treat minor wounds, burns, and sunburns, as well as conditions like psoriasis and dermatitis.
